jokes.text: What? We can not sit on the couch without something happens?
jokes.setup: Character's frustration about not being able to sit peacefully
jokes.punchline: Meta-reference to the show's running couch gag tradition
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta-joke about the show's format works well as an opener

jokes.text: We drove 18 hours to come here. But on the other hand, this barrier is very sweet!
jokes.setup: Visitor complaining about museum being closed to them
jokes.punchline: Sudden positive pivot to admiring the barrier itself
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor in the character's abrupt mood change

jokes.text: A feast! A feast! A feast! Homer, you work with these people. A little restraint. Moderation! Moderation! Moderation!
jokes.setup: Homer getting excited about food at company event
jokes.punchline: Marge's plea for moderation delivered with equal repetitive intensity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's hypocrisy in demanding moderation immoderately

jokes.text: I am your supervisor. - Really? How do I do?
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to be Homer's supervisor
jokes.punchline: Homer innocently asking for performance feedback
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious earnestness creates perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: Lisa, Mr. Burns is the adult the sweetest I know. You know how many adults? Basically, he and Dad.
jokes.setup: Character defending Burns as sweet
jokes.punchline: Reveals extremely limited sample size
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - setup seems reasonable until the devastating reveal

jokes.text: Thank you, I bought for my funeral. There is no rear, then I do not ask me back. I paid for a coffin Molton, I bong and blood want to feel it.
jokes.setup: Moe being complimented on his smoking jacket
jokes.punchline: Reveals morbid planning and expensive funeral preparations
jokes.explanation: Perfectly fits Moe's depressive character with absurd funeral economics

jokes.text: type in a Burns giant head.
jokes.setup: Educational museum presentation
jokes.punchline: Burns egomaniacally inserting his giant head into historical narrative
jokes.explanation: Visual absurdity of Burns' narcissistic museum display

jokes.text: A wooden plane! It was time that trees are used to something. Rather than keep you planted straight as morons.
jokes.setup: Admiring wooden aircraft construction
jokes.punchline: Burns' bizarre hostility toward trees for just standing there
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - Burns finding moral fault with trees for being trees

jokes.text: It flew at an altitude of 2mètres, a distance of 1m50! Then it was discovered that the rain Starting in the flames. Then the Führer me fired.
jokes.setup: Describing his wooden plane's modest flight achievements
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Hitler fired him for this failure
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation works perfectly - Burns casually dropping Nazi employment history

jokes.text: And we can use toilets of the future. Homer was a reactor!
jokes.setup: Listing benefits of the fancy event
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about Homer being a nuclear reactor
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist moment that doesn't quite land cohesively

jokes.text: Entering into force immediately the coverage plan Social workers ... ... Is abolished! Year after year, the health service ... bla bla bla, forms of politeness, Out!
jokes.setup: Burns announcing important company news at celebration
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of health benefits with literal 'bla bla bla'
jokes.explanation: Burns' callous delivery and literal 'bla bla bla' perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Fly, stupid! I tried to explain that this machine does not really flying. Smithers, he must believe!
jokes.setup: Burns trying to escape in his wooden plane
jokes.punchline: Demanding Smithers maintain the illusion despite obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the wooden plane setup with Burns' delusional persistence

jokes.text: He is married. Change of course Carole must be notified.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering if coworkers will flirt
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact concern for proper notification protocol
jokes.explanation: Marge's moral conscientiousness delivered with bureaucratic precision

jokes.text: Think team 'Friends' looking for a new player? This series is not even broadcast! Gee, I have been perfect in the role Irish cousin of Rachel!
jokes.setup: Homer needing a new job
jokes.punchline: Delusional confidence about joining an ended TV show as oddly specific character
jokes.explanation: Homer's specific delusion about being 'Irish cousin of Rachel' is wonderfully absurd

jokes.text: As long as I do not work more than 5h by day, without lifting heavy weight. No job at the church, nor as Father Christmas in the nuts ... With people who enjoy nudity.
jokes.setup: Homer listing reasonable job requirements
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and bizarre restrictions ending with nudity clause
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from reasonable to absurd job restrictions

jokes.text: Children go to the pharmacy of your parents. And find the pills marked: Lithium dibromide. And send them to me. Now! Nobody likes Clown lunatics!
jokes.setup: TV personality addressing children
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate request for psychiatric medication with self-aware justification
jokes.explanation: Dark humor works well - Krusty's desperation leading to inappropriate children's TV content

jokes.text: I treat with Ralph pills from criminal evidence! I have desires smuggling mad!
jokes.setup: Wiggum discussing alternative treatments
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to evidence tampering and criminal desires
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - incompetent law enforcement with cheerful confession

jokes.text: I like big buttocks, and I can not deny it.
jokes.setup: Corporate pharmaceutical cheerleaders
jokes.punchline: Adapting hip-hop song for drug marketing
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much additional comedic twist

jokes.text: And in these other countries, we sit the ground to eat insects. In which country? Have you ever visited Norway? No. Well, this is the case in Norway.
jokes.setup: Executive defending high drug prices by citing foreign conditions
jokes.punchline: Completely false claim about Norway that he doubles down on
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate BS - confident lies about easily verifiable facts

jokes.text: Wait, it's not a drug! is a cashew nut! I have a cork bottle. Mine flew.
jokes.setup: Elderly patients examining their medication
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre substitutes for actual medicine
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of what passes for medication in cost-cutting measures

jokes.text: Your pills have become very expensive. And as everyone is sheet you it was decided to let you die. For those who survive this night, Tomorrow morning there will be waffles.
jokes.setup: Administrator explaining cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: Casual death sentence followed by cheerful waffle promise
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - bureaucratic callousness with absurd incentive

jokes.text: I did not survive the 2nd World War and to die! I will fight! It is crazy, but do you want, it is young!
jokes.setup: Grampa rallying against the nursing home policy
jokes.punchline: Illogical war survival logic followed by others dismissing him as young and crazy
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa moment with typical confused logic and age dynamics

jokes.text: The border? I should do remove my fingerprints. I could wear gloves. No, they are upstairs.
jokes.setup: Grampa considering border crossing illegally
jokes.punchline: Overthinking criminal preparation then defeated by simple logistics
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Grampa's criminal planning thwarted by basic laziness

jokes.text: We're gringos come muchos dineros spend in your country. Beautiful! Welcome to Canada.
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to speak Spanish at Canadian border
jokes.punchline: Complete geographical confusion accepted enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical cultural confusion paired with Canadian politeness

jokes.text: (Now, without Celine Dion) (Veterans of a single War)
jokes.setup: Welcome to Canada sign
jokes.punchline: Sign advertises Canada is now Celine Dion-free and mocks limited war experience
jokes.explanation: Good Canadian stereotype humor with specific cultural references

jokes.text: It is good Johnny. Johnny thank you a thousand times. As a gift, here's a DVD player. What is ... Where there is syrup?
jokes.setup: Homer giving gift to Canadian contact
jokes.punchline: Expecting maple syrup dispenser in DVD player based on Canadian stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Standard stereotype humor but fits Homer's character logic

jokes.text: Canadian? Looking for a fight? Finally, I mean, just you see!
jokes.setup: Homer taking offense at being called Canadian
jokes.punchline: Immediate backdown when challenged to actually fight
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer cowardice pattern but executed competently

jokes.text: I declare that you have the most beautiful brown eyes that are! And I think you hide ... a heart as big that our borders! Now déguerpissez! Grand crazy.
jokes.setup: Border guard asking about declarations
jokes.punchline: Homer's romantic deflection followed by guard's abrupt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Homer's charm offensive working unexpectedly well

jokes.text: He had a passport? Yes ... for my heart.
jokes.setup: Guard questioning lack of documentation
jokes.punchline: Homer's romantic metaphor treating emotions as travel documents
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Homer's mix of stupidity and unexpected poetry

jokes.text: I have pills to make you sleep, eliminate clot, copulating. In that order!
jokes.setup: Homer offering medications to elderly people
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sequencing of medical effects
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable humor about elderly sexuality and medication

jokes.text: Look, the same D and for Dumbo Drop ... Incredibly, only three have never been purchased.
jokes.setup: Grampa showing off commemorative jacket
jokes.punchline: Pride in owning merchandise from universally rejected movie
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor with Grampa's misplaced pride

jokes.text: With Xanax, I am no longer anxious to be a Simpsons! I just afraid of being hooked the Xanax, but it made me forget.
jokes.setup: Patient celebrating anxiety relief
jokes.punchline: New anxiety about addiction that medication makes them forget
jokes.explanation: Clever circular logic about anxiety medication creating and solving problems

jokes.text: My 8 babies make me crazy! they cough, sneezes, and make noise! They fear my concierge who dares get out!
jokes.setup: Apu explaining why he needs medication
jokes.punchline: Children so problematic they've terrorized building management
jokes.explanation: Fits Apu's many children running gag with escalating domestic chaos

jokes.text: I do not even understand my bad jokes! I'm tired.
jokes.setup: Apu continuing to explain his exhaustion
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment that his own humor doesn't make sense
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about comedy writing itself delivered through character exhaustion

jokes.text: What did you do for me from giving me $ 5000 yesterday?
jokes.setup: Homer considering helping Flanders
jokes.punchline: Expecting payment for theoretical future favor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - transactional approach to hypothetical generosity

jokes.text: If you let us come, I leave you Only 10 minutes with my squishy machine. You do what you want ... No camera?
jokes.setup: Flanders offering incentive for Homer's help
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate equipment loan with suspicious privacy requirement
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo humor that's more awkward than clever

jokes.text: I told her it was just courage to believe in a false God. I do not believe in one God. Rather, a super team of gods.
jokes.setup: Religious discussion between characters
jokes.punchline: Homer's polytheistic superhero team concept
jokes.explanation: Creative take on religious pluralism through Homer's comic book logic

jokes.text: Where is she gone, ta super team? I'll tell you which is the true God, but only if you stay calm.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Homer's god theory
jokes.punchline: Homer's conditional revelation with impossible calm requirement
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to previous joke with Homer's typical impossible conditions

jokes.text: Tabernacle! Ca c't'un criss' heap of pills! For a new Vietnam?
jokes.setup: Canadian seeing large quantity of pills
jokes.punchline: French-Canadian swearing followed by Vietnam War reference
jokes.explanation: Regional humor with Canadian dialect and confused historical reference

jokes.text: Say you want to draw a Taffe on my joint? It's legal here. I was warned that Satan would be charming. Here we go!
jokes.setup: Canadian offering marijuana to visitors
jokes.punchline: Flanders' immediate Satan identification and panic
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Canadian casualness vs Flanders' religious panic

jokes.text: No, no, it's homemade. Attention is even hotter a dispatch from Fox News. Wait, let me a t'enrouler wet towel around his head.
jokes.setup: Canadian warning about hot coffee
jokes.punchline: Fox News temperature comparison with practical head-wrapping solution
jokes.explanation: Media satire with absurd practical solution but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: My buttocks are huge when I'm handcuffed.
jokes.setup: Homer being arrested and processed
jokes.punchline: Specific body image concern during arrest
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer-esque vanity concern at inappropriate moment

jokes.text: I am a big fat french stupid.
jokes.setup: Homer forced to admit something by Canadian authorities
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating confession in broken French
jokes.explanation: Simple humiliation humor with language barrier comedy

jokes.text: Fortunately, we have not put your name on the front!
jokes.setup: Looking at commemorative jacket after disaster
jokes.punchline: Relief about anonymity in failed merchandise
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier jacket joke with practical silver lining

jokes.text: For the first time I can say without being drunk ... I am really proud of you!
jokes.setup: Grampa expressing rare pride in Homer
jokes.punchline: Qualifier that he's usually drunk when expressing positive emotions
jokes.explanation: Dark family dynamic humor with excellent economy and character insight

jokes.text: For the first time I can say without being drunk ... I am really proud of you!
jokes.setup: Lisa expresses pride in Homer's drug smuggling efforts
jokes.punchline: Qualifies that she's sober while saying it, implying past drunk praise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with dark implication about Lisa's drinking habits

jokes.text: It is illegal to drink on the road. But it went where? Let good ...
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out drinking laws to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Grampa's casual dismissal and location-based confusion
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly confusion humor without much innovation

jokes.text: Sir, my thyroid swells. Stop that! I need your throat for my speech.
jokes.setup: Smithers reporting medical problem
jokes.punchline: Burns treating employee's throat as personal property
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns characterization - treating employee illness as inconvenience to his needs

jokes.text: Hang Smithers. I will stir heaven and earth to save you. It is always easier than explaining another my filing system.
jokes.setup: Burns vowing to save Smithers' life
jokes.punchline: Reveals purely selfish motivation about administrative convenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - grand gesture undermined by petty bureaucratic concern

jokes.text: Katherine Hepburn! But no, poor fool! Burns is me. Follow me.
jokes.setup: Burns revealing his airplane pilot abilities
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity comparison followed by imperious self-identification
jokes.explanation: Random Burns non-sequitur that's more eccentric than funny

jokes.text: Finally, my plane has fulfilled its original goal ... Overturn U.S. laws!
jokes.setup: Burns using his plane for drug smuggling
jokes.punchline: Reveals plane was always intended for illegal purposes
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes earlier airplane exposition

jokes.text: That's why you have balls snow, and the H-bomb!
jokes.setup: Burns insulting Canadian courage
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical comparison between snowballs and nuclear weapons
jokes.explanation: Burns' confused nationalism with bizarre military logic

jokes.text: I always wanted to see a man with the IQ of a child to enforce by the government.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining his interest in helping Homer
jokes.punchline: Scientific curiosity about governmental incompetence
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about Homer representing government incompetence

jokes.text: Really? In America, is four times a week.
jokes.setup: Response to comment about not liking governmental incompetence
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual frequency estimate for American incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware political humor delivered through Homer's matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: There is a 3 parachutes. It is for me ... And the other 2 are gifts for my nephews.
jokes.setup: Plane emergency requiring parachutes
jokes.punchline: Burns claiming all parachutes as personal/family property
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns selfishness in life-or-death situation with absurd gift logic

jokes.text: Homer, you're sure Learn steer it? I get both we have wings!
jokes.setup: Questioning Homer's piloting ability
jokes.punchline: Homer's confidence based on anatomical comparison to birds
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical flawed logic applying biological features to mechanical skills

jokes.text: You are under arrest. For what reason? To have scared a policeman!
jokes.setup: Police arrest without clear cause
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of arresting someone for police fear
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist legal logic that satirizes police authority

jokes.text: Thanks to the pills immunisantes against the lactose I can drink Krusty the milkshake. Disgraceful!
jokes.setup: Patient celebrating medical breakthrough
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's moral outrage at lactose tolerance treatment
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's misplaced moral priorities create good character comedy

jokes.text: If you can not safe, join them! Chief, I watched in the manual. Many of your quotes did not appear at all.
jokes.setup: Wiggum citing police manual philosophy
jokes.punchline: Lou fact-checking reveals Wiggum makes up his quotes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum characterization - authoritative incompetence exposed by subordinate

jokes.text: Only my soul and my breath can save him.
jokes.setup: Burns attempting to save dying Smithers
jokes.punchline: Claiming mystical healing powers rather than using medicine
jokes.explanation: Burns' ego extending to supernatural healing abilities

jokes.text: M Burns made me a freelance consultant!
jokes.setup: Burns announces full health coverage for employees
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals he's now a freelancer, presumably without benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - Homer gets screwed by technicality just as benefits are restored
